rationale: Load-bearing hooks for the runtime 'priced-or-it-doesnt-ship' serving-admission gate (docs/approved/priced-or-it-doesnt-ship.md, issue #1016 v1). The gate is a thin per-route injection (one guarded call + early return) into 5 upstream-shaped service forwarders; each touch is small and compiles clean if dropped, so an upstream merge or refactor can silently revert it and re-open the catch-all $0-billing hole. These sentinels pin the gate companion, its R3-consistency predicate, the DI wiring, every per-route hook, the setting constant, and the enabled-set getter so preflight + the upstream-merge PR shape check fail loudly if any disappears.

sentinels.rationale: The gate core: pure decision (tkPricedServingGateRejected), full path that writes the 404 + fires the reject-time alert (tkCheckPricedServingGate), and the per-platform 404 body writer. The literal error envelope strings (not_found_error for Anthropic, invalid_request_error + model_not_priced subcode for OpenAI/NewAPI) are the byte-aligned shapes clients depend on; losing them silently changes the rejection contract. The NotifyPricingMissing call is v1's automatic-pricing channel (reject → Feishu card → ops hot-fixes the price) and satisfies design R4 (the gate is never an empty no-op).

R3 predicate consistency (design §7). The gate MUST use tkIsModelEffectivelyPriced, NOT bare IsModelPriced: a catalog entry whose token price is present-but-zero is a catalog member (IsModelPriced=true) but billing's GetModelPricing resolves it to ErrModelPricingUnavailable and charges $0 — a gate on bare membership would be form-over-substance. The media-price clause keeps legitimately media-priced models (zero token price, non-zero per-second/per-image) passing. If this predicate is deleted or downgraded to bare membership, the gate silently re-opens the $0-billing hole it exists to close.

Post-construction DI setters (mirroring SetPricingAvailabilityService) and the per-service one-liner wrappers each route hook calls. The gemini compat service holds none of the deps natively, so its SetPricedServingGateDeps injects all three at once. Dropping a setter dead-codes the gate for that service (silently off in prod while unit tests pass); dropping a wrapper breaks the route-hook call sites.

Route hook 1/5 — OpenAI native /v1/responses (+chat). Inserted right after billingModel := account.GetMappedModel, BEFORE upstream forward / SSE stream start (cannot 404 mid-stream). A silent revert re-opens $0 serving for unpriced OpenAI models when openai joins the enabled set.
Route hook 2/5 — responses-bridge (ForwardAsResponses). Inserted after anthropicReq.Model = mappedModel, before forward / stream start.
Route hook 3/5 — chat-completions-bridge (ForwardAsChatCompletions). Inserted after anthropicReq.Model = mappedModel, before forward / stream start.

Route hooks 4/5 + 5/5 — gemini compat Forward AND ForwardNative (the digest-chain dual paths; BOTH must gate, the shared hook literal appears twice in this file). This is the first-launch enabled platform (gemini/Vertex), so a revert here is the highest-impact: it re-opens the exact catch-all $0 hole this design targets. The struct field anchor proves the post-construction-injected deps slot survives (the gemini compat service holds none of them on its upstream constructor, so the field is the only evidence the SetPricedServingGateDeps target still exists).

Route hook (native anthropic /v1/messages catch-all). Inserted right after the deprecated-model gate (post-mapping, pre-forward) — the native catch-all (empty model_mapping = allow-all) is the原始 vulnerability重灾区. anthropic is NOT in the first-launch enabled set but joins per the rollout plan; the hook must survive until then.

The setting key for the per-platform enabled SET. Renaming/removing it silently disables every IsPricedServingGateEnabled lookup (gate off for all platforms) without a compile error if a stale string literal lingers elsewhere.

must_contain: func (s *SettingService) IsPricedServingGateEnabled(ctx context.Context, platform string) bool
sentinels.rationale: The enabled-set getter (comma-separated SET membership, fail-open-toward-off) and the first-launch default (gemini/Vertex, D2). Losing the getter breaks the gate's enable check; losing the cold-start default means a fresh DB ships with the gate OFF, regressing the 'gemini/Vertex one-step ON' launch contract.
